note: sahadeva+ca real split (sandhi visarga+c->śc); head=ca 'and' is a stray particle, not a true compound head
śveta+uṣṇīṣa, sandhi a+u->o matches; 'white turban' correct bahuvrihi sense
a+ula drops the 't' of ātula (true a+tula 'matchless'); head gloss itself flagged '?' (jackal), unrelated
tris+niśā correct (sandhi s+n->r); but niśā='night' mistranslated as 'sharp' (confused with niśita)
div+aj+alpa garbled; true divyakalpa=divya+kalpa; chain 'heaven+drove out+little' is nonsense
cintayitum arhasi is two words, not a compound, but roots cint/arh correctly recovered; 'ought to think' conveyed
upakaraṇa derives from plain upa+kṛ (no s-augment); 'upaskf' is a different real root wrongly picked, but gloss 'much help' still conveys correct sense
aha+ibhya shows no a+i->e sandhi (hiatus kept), implausible boundary; chain 'day+noble' incoherent
tilaśas+kṛ matches source 'tilaśaḥ kṛta' exactly; 'cut into pieces+made' correct
śuc/śoka+atiga plausible root split; atiga='surpassing/overcoming' loosely rendered as 'not heeding'
svap+ram does not reconstruct subrata (=su+vrata); 'sleeps+rejoice' unrelated to 'good vow'
triśiras+man, sandhi -as+m->-om matches; 'Trishiras considered/regarded' reasonable
pA+u+u garbles paurava/pauravāḥ, doubled meaningless 'u'; chain 'protect+and+and' nonsense
garbled decomposition of śakrapurogama; misses real members śakra/puraḥ/gama; chain incoherent
pf+i+vf omits the 'd' of purīdvāra entirely, does not reconstruct surface; chain unrelated to 'city gate'
mahāsena+as doesn't cleanly reconstruct trailing -sa; 'Mahasena is' is not a coherent compound gloss
BU+avani+vf inserts spurious 'avani'(earth); true bhāvanirvṛtti=bhāva+nirvṛtti; chain nonsensical
BA+o+i+ca garbled, misses bhānu (sun) entirely; head=ca stray particle, chain incoherent
wrong boundary śatadhā+ṛ instead of real śata+dhāra ('hundred-edged'); chain vaguely evokes hundred+clashing but literal sense wrong
u+vf unrelated to real phrase 'avyo vāreṣu' (sheep-wool sieves); chain 'and+surrounded' unrelated
wrong boundary ca+apahasta instead of real cāpa+hasta ('bow in hand'); head coincidentally glossed 'bow' but 'hand' lost and 'and' spurious
na+ucchvas, sandhi a+u->o matches; 'not breathing' correct
rāma+ambā dual with correct fem-ā dual shortening before -yoḥ; 'mother' correct but 'ram' mistranslated as verb 'rejoice' instead of name Rāma
āvis+kṛ matches source 'āviḥ kṛṇoti'; 'made manifest' correct
av+day does not correspond to the possessive suffix -vant in prabhāsvant; chain incoherent
a+maT does not correspond to pronoun stem asmad ('our'); chain 'oh+stole+said' unrelated to 'from our speech'
tA+yaj+a is a false etymology; real taijasa derives from tejas, not yaj; head=a stray particle, chain incoherent
dual ending -au mistaken for separate word 'u'='and'; samrāj='sovereign' correct but ending mistranslated as conjunction
tā+camū matches source 'tāṃ camūm' (sandhi m+c->ṃc); 'that army' correct sense
pA+u+as same garbling pattern as id12, doesn't reconstruct paura; chain nonsensical
mah(ān)+doṣa matches 'mahāndoṣa'; 'great evil/fault' correct
aS+aNga drops the -ṭā- of aṣṭau, truncates the numeral 'eight'; chain 'attained+body' misses 'eight limbs' sense
muc->moka lacks the expected ṣ of mokṣa, incoherent compound sense; likely OCR-mangled mṛgājina ('deerskin'); chain incoherent
loses sambhāra/sandhāyin entirely, replaced by unrelated bhū/ras/dhī roots; chain incoherent
dru+avarza drops the 'm' of druma, does not reconstruct surface; chain unrelated to 'tree/rain'
vṛjina+agra, sandhi a+a->A matches; 'crooked+top/beginning' reasonable compound sense
spurious filler 'a'='oh' inserted as a member, missing the sam- prefix of samanvita; 'yoga+follows' loosely conveys 'endowed with'
a+i+ahita loses āditya ('sun') entirely and yields ahita ('hostile'), opposite of true sahita ('accompanied by'); chain wrong/inverted
dual ending -au mistaken for separate word 'u'='and', same pattern as id27; unmukha as 'eager/desirous' plausible secondary sense, but head 'and' spurious
wrong boundary turns hīna into ahīna, inverting the meaning (true vedādhikārahīna = 'devoid of right to Veda'); also garbles adhikāra into aDika+f
